reasoning: I'm voting for Shantanu Thakur again, though I won't pretend I'm doing it with the conviction I had in 2019. Mamata's system is irredeemably extractive—my facilitation fees at Petrapole prove that every day—and Biswajit Das switching parties like musical chairs is exactly the rot I despise on both sides. The Left and Congress offer nothing new. Modi's framework is still the only institutional direction worth betting on, even though he hasn't dismantled the patronage system the way I hoped. I'm not voting for what BJP has delivered; I'm voting against what Mamata represents, and for what Modi's framework still promises to eventually build.
primary_drivers: Mamata's governance is demonstrably worse and extractive
Modi's institutional framework remains the only credible direction despite disappointing execution
No viable alternative—Biswajit Das, Left Front, Congress offer nothing better
